race.name: Trek 100
race.tagline: Annual charity bike ride through Wisconsin's rolling farmland benefiting childhood cancer research.

vitals.location: Trek HQ, 801 West Madison Street, Waterloo, Wisconsin 53594

climate.overview: Typical June weather in southern Wisconsin features mild temperatures and variable conditions suitable for cycling through farmland.

terrain.overview: Paved secondary roads through rolling dairy farmland in Jefferson, Columbia, Dane, and Dodge Counties with challenging hillsides and flats; MTB option on private singletrack trails at Trek HQ.

logistics.explanation: Well-supported charity ride at Trek HQ with staggered starts, rest stops, and post-ride party, though open to traffic.

length.explanation: Multiple distances from 24 to 100 miles plus MTB laps cater to all levels.

technicality.explanation: Primarily paved roads with rolling terrain; MTB adds moderate singletrack.

elevation.explanation: Challenging hills on longer routes with 3,000-3,900 ft gain on 100-mile option.

climate.explanation: Pleasant early summer conditions in Wisconsin farmland.

adventure.explanation: Scenic farmland loops with some hills and new fun segments.

prestige.explanation: Established charity event with $22M+ raised, tied to Trek's legacy.

race_quality.explanation: High production as non-competitive supported ride from Trek.

experience.explanation: Festive atmosphere with party, pancakes, and heroic cause.

community.explanation: Strong charity focus for MACC Fund unites riders for kids.

field_depth.explanation: Inclusive charity ride, not elite racing field.

value.explanation: Low reg fees with fundraising; massive impact per dollar.

cultural_impact.explanation: Iconic Trek event celebrating 50 years, major charity staple.

biased_opinion.overview: The Trek 100 stands out as a feel-good charity powerhouse, blending accessible routes, Trek's polish, and a noble cause—perfect for community-minded riders seeking fun over suffering.

final_verdict.one_liner: Premier charity ride with heart, hills, and headquarters party.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Trek 100 delivers a polished, inclusive cycling experience through Wisconsin's picturesque farmland, raising millions for childhood cancer research while offering diverse distances and a festive vibe at Trek HQ. Ideal for fundraisers who want challenge without cutthroat competition.

logistics.overview: Fully supported ride with staggered starts (7:30am for 100/62mi, 9:30am for shorter), rest stops, volunteers, and law enforcement at intersections; roads open to traffic; arrive 1hr early; post-ride party.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels in Waterloo, Lake Mills, or Madison; camping not specified.
logistics.registration_notes: $50-75 reg fee + fundraising minimums ($50-200); open now; ride-from-home option; tax-deductible pledges.

history.overview: Annual charity ride since 1990 benefiting MACC Fund; over $22M raised; 2025 marks Trek's 50th anniversary.
history.founder: Trek Bicycle (in partnership with MACC Fund)
notable_moments: 2025: 50th Trek anniversary; new 25mi loop and MTB course

course_description.character: Rolling paved roads through dairy farmland with quiet secondary roads, challenging hillsides, flats, and fun segments; new loop-style courses; MTB on private moderate singletrack (10mi laps, no jumps).
course_description.signature_challenge: Hilly 100-mile route with 3,889ft elevation through beautiful Wisconsin countryside.
